name: input-group
title: Input group
description: Composes the Commons Input with a prefix and/or suffix into a single bordered control: text addons (units, currency, protocol), leading/trailing icons, and trailing action buttons (clear, copy, visibility toggle). One border wraps prefix + input + suffix, one focus ring appears when the input is focused, and aria-invalid styles the whole group border. Field-wired, RTL-aware via logical properties, and forced-colors safe.
useWhen: Showing a unit, currency symbol, or protocol beside a value as part of one control (e.g. $ amount, lbs, https://)
Adding a trailing action to a text field (clear the value, copy it, toggle password visibility)
Pairing a leading or trailing icon with an input inside a single bordered field
avoidWhen: A plain field with only a decorative symbol (use Input's own prefix/suffix)
Choosing from a set of options (use select, radio-group, or combobox)
The addon is the primary action of a form (use a separate labeled Button)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the text input; Shift+Tab moves focus away backwards
Trailing action buttons are individual tab stops after the input
Prefix/suffix text and icons are decorative and non-interactive — they are not tab stops
Clicking the group's padding or a decorative addon focuses the input
A disabled group removes the input (and its actions) from the tab order
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (the group is min-block-size 2.75rem; action buttons fill the height and are min 44px wide)
notes: One border wraps prefix + input + suffix as a single visual control; the focus ring is scoped to the input (has-[input:focus]) so a focused action button rings only itself.

Decorative prefix/suffix are aria-hidden so a repeated symbol does not pollute the input's accessible name; a MEANINGFUL unit is surfaced via prefixLabel/suffixLabel, rendered visually-hidden and linked with aria-describedby.
aria-invalid (from a Field or explicit prop) styles the WHOLE group border with the error token via data-invalid, and error state is border + ring, never color alone.
Action buttons are real <button>s (via InputGroupButton) with required accessible names; they are never aria-hidden.
Logical properties place prefix at the inline-start and suffix at the inline-end, mirroring under dir=rtl; a visible border is kept in every state for forced-colors mode.
